title: Berlin Transport Strike - Quick Summary
items: **Do not treat a Berlin transport strike as one broken city.** First find out which layer is affected: BVG, S-Bahn, regional rail, airport routes or buses.
**Make the day smaller before you make it harder.** If the map looks messy, choose one area of Berlin and do it well instead of dragging a broken route across town.
**In the centre, walking can be the better move.** Alexanderplatz, Museum Island, Hackescher Markt and Unter den Linden are close enough that a delay can become a useful route.
**Move earlier for hard deadlines.** BER Airport, Hauptbahnhof, timed tickets and tour starts need a backup before the platform gets crowded.
**Use backups in pieces.** One working train line, one short walk, then a taxi only if it really helps is often better than forcing one perfect route.
